title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
summary: A four-layer architecture for maintaining consistent brand voice in LLM applications: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach addresses the fragility of single-instruction prompts at scale by decomposing voice consistency into specialized layers, each handling different aspects of tone preservation.
writeup: This talk presents a four-layer architecture for keeping brand voice consistent in LLM applications: an immutable identity layer, a situational mode layer, an example-anchored voice layer, and a deterministic post-generation veto. It matters because single-instruction tone prompts grow fragile at scale, and decomposing voice into specialized layers lets each handle a distinct aspect of tone preservation. The core takeaway: treat voice consistency as a structured system rather than one prompt, with a deterministic final check vetoing outputs after generation.
